mnemonics: P(A and B) = P(A) * P(B) if A and B are independent
P(A) = P(A and B) / P(B) if A and B are independent
P(A) = P(A) * P(B) / P(A and B) if A and B are not independent
Bayes' Rule: P(A|B) = P(A and B) / P(B)
keyPoints: Probability Notation: P(A) = Probability of event A
Inference using Full Joint Distributions: P(A and B) = Joint Probability of A and B
Independence: P(A and B) = P(A) * P(B) if A and B are independent
Bayes' Rule: Used to update probabilities based on new information
Baye's Rule: P(A|B) = P(A and B) / P(B) is used to calculate conditional probability
